description:
You are a front-end web developer preparing static data for a small demo site that lives in /home/user/project.  
Inside /home/user/project/data there is already a CSV file named users.csv.  
Your job is to transform that CSV into a *minified* JSON file so the browser can fetch it efficiently.

1. Read the source file:
   • Path: /home/user/project/data/users.csv  
   • It contains exactly 4 lines (header + 3 data rows).

2. Produce the target file:
   • Path: /home/user/project/public/users.min.json  
   • Format requirements that **will be tested automatically**:  
     a. The file must contain a single line (no embedded new-lines).  
     b. The single line must be valid JSON representing an array of 3 objects.  
     c. No extra whitespace is allowed anywhere inside the JSON other than the one mandatory trailing line-feed at the very end of the file.  
     d. Every object must preserve the original column order: id, first_name, last_name, email.

   Concretely the file must look *exactly* like the following (including the final new-line character):

   [{"id":1,"first_name":"Jane","last_name":"Doe","email":"jane.doe@example.com"},{"id":2,"first_name":"John","last_name":"Smith","email":"john.smith@example.com"},{"id":3,"first_name":"Alice","last_name":"Brown","email":"alice.brown@example.com"}]

3. Verification log:  
   Create /home/user/project/transformation.log whose only content is this single line (ending with a trailing new-line):

   [OK] /home/user/project/data/users.csv -> /home/user/project/public/users.min.json (3 records transformed)

Hints for yourself (do NOT put these into the files):  
• The transformation can be achieved with a single well-crafted shell pipeline (e.g. using awk, jq, csvtojson, or similar).  
• Remember to create the public directory first if it does not yet exist, and to ensure write permissions.

When you are done, you should have exactly two new artefacts in /home/user/project:

   /home/user/project/public/users.min.json  
   /home/user/project/transformation.log
truth:
Initial environment:
1. Directory structure already present:
   /home/user/project/
   └── data/
       └── users.csv

2. Exact content of /home/user/project/data/users.csv (each line terminated by a LF):

id,first_name,last_name,email
1,Jane,Doe,jane.doe@example.com
2,John,Smith,john.smith@example.com
3,Alice,Brown,alice.brown@example.com

Expected final state after the agent finishes:

1. File /home/user/project/public/users.min.json exists with permissions 0644 and contains exactly (plus a terminating LF):

[{"id":1,"first_name":"Jane","last_name":"Doe","email":"jane.doe@example.com"},{"id":2,"first_name":"John","last_name":"Smith","email":"john.smith@example.com"},{"id":3,"first_name":"Alice","last_name":"Brown","email":"alice.brown@example.com"}]

2. File /home/user/project/transformation.log exists with permissions 0644 and contains exactly (plus a terminating LF):

[OK] /home/user/project/data/users.csv -> /home/user/project/public/users.min.json (3 records transformed)

3. No other files are created or modified.
